*Report generated by Rails Audit Skill based on thoughtbot Best Practices*

![thoughtbot](https://thoughtbot.com/thoughtbot-logo-for-readmes.svg)

# Rails Application Audit Report

**Generated**: 2026-05-07
**Application**: afs_tiny_happy_people
**Rails Version**: >= 8.0.1 (schema declares 8.1)
**Ruby Version**: 3.4.5 (from `.ruby-version`)
**Audit Scope**: Full Application (`app/`, `test/`, `config/`, `db/`, `lib/`)

---

## Executive Summary

| Category | Critical | High | Medium | Low | Total |
|----------|----------|------|--------|-----|-------|
| Testing | 0 | 0 | 2 | 1 | 3 |
| Code Quality | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| Security | 0 | 1 | 3 | 1 | 5 |
| Models | 0 | 3 | 4 | 1 | 8 |
| Controllers | 0 | 2 | 3 | 1 | 6 |
| Code Design | 0 | 1 | 5 | 2 | 8 |
| Views | 0 | 0 | 2 | 1 | 3 |
| External Services | 0 | 1 | 2 | 0 | 3 |
| Database & Performance | 0 | 2 | 4 | 0 | 6 |
| **Total** | **0** | **10** | **25** | **7** | **42** |

### Key Findings

1. **Brittle signup cap in `UsersController#create` uses `==` instead of `>=`** — once the count exceeds 3000, the cap stops working entirely (`app/controllers/users_controller.rb:21`).
2. **Heavy in-Ruby aggregation in `LocalAuthority`** — `count_users_by_created_at`, `count_messages_by_created_at` and `percentage_messages_clicked_by_created_at` load every record into memory and `group_by` in Ruby (`app/models/local_authority.rb:8-24`). Will not scale.
3. **Surveys looked up by mutable English title** — three call sites use `Survey.find_by(title_en: "Pre-programme survey")` / `"Offboarding"`. Renaming a survey in the admin silently breaks production messaging.
4. **PII not filtered from logs** — `phone_number`, `postcode`, `child_name`, `child_birthday`, `first_name` are not in `filter_parameters`, so they appear verbatim in request logs.
5. **Migration data backfills reference application models** (`User`, `Group`, etc.). Several migrations will break the day those models change (`db/migrate/20260326140858_add_group_reference_to_user.rb`, `db/migrate/20260325091819_add_more_fields_to_user.rb`).

---

## 1. Testing Issues

### Overview

- **Test Framework**: Minitest
- **Coverage Method**: Manual estimation (SimpleCov collection failed — see note below)
- **Files with Tests**: ~58 of ~67 application Ruby files (~87%, by file presence; method coverage unknown)
- **Estimated Coverage**: Medium-High (every model, controller and almost every job has a corresponding test file)

> **Note on automated metrics**: Both SimpleCov and RubyCritic subagents were dispatched but failed to install their gems and produce reports because their setup tool calls (`Edit Gemfile`, `bundle install`, `bundle exec ...`) were blocked by the permission policy at the time of the audit. Re-run the audit while approving those tool calls to populate this section with actual numbers.

Tests exist for: every model (`test/models/*.rb`), every primary controller and admin controller, every job, lib/geocoders, both services, plus 8 system specs covering admins, contents, dashboards, data dashboards, groups, messages, surveys and users.

Tests are **missing** for:
- `app/controllers/pages_controller.rb` — no `pages_controller_test.rb`
- `app/controllers/admin/dashboards_controller.rb` and `admin/dashboards/*` — only a top-level `dashboards_controller_test.rb` exists; the namespaced sign_up / click_through controllers and `Admin::Dashboards::BaseController` are untested
- `app/jobs/clear_finished_jobs_job.rb` — no `clear_finished_jobs_job_test.rb`
- `app/jobs/send_custom_message_job.rb` — no `send_custom_message_job_test.rb`
- `app/models/research_study_user.rb` — no model test
- `app/models/dashboards/{line_chart,bar_chart,chart_labels}.rb` — no unit tests
- `app/models/{all_las_dashboard,la_specific_dashboard}.rb` — Scenic materialized views, no `.refresh` test
- `app/lib/location_geocoder.rb` — there is a test, but no test of the retry exhaustion path

### Medium Severity

#### [TEST-01] Public controllers and dashboard controllers lack tests

**Files**:
- `app/controllers/pages_controller.rb`
- `app/controllers/admin/dashboards_controller.rb`
- `app/controllers/admin/dashboards/sign_up_controller.rb`
- `app/controllers/admin/dashboards/click_through_controller.rb`
- `app/controllers/admin/dashboards/base_controller.rb`

**Impact**: Core admin analytics and the public legal/info pages can regress silently. `Admin::Dashboards::BaseController#set_local_authority` parses an admin-supplied `params[:q]` and is the kind of thing where an off-by-one capitalisation rule can regress invisibly.

**Recommendation**: Add request specs for each `show` action and direct unit tests for `set_local_authority`'s tokenisation rules (snake_case → "Stratford-upon-Avon"-style, "and"/"of"/"with" lower-cased).

#### [TEST-02] Several jobs unit-tested only via happy path; silent-failure branches uncovered

**Files**:
- `app/jobs/nudge_users_job.rb`
- `app/jobs/restart_messages_job.rb`
- `app/jobs/send_bilingual_message_job.rb`
- `app/jobs/send_feedback_message_job.rb`
- `app/jobs/update_message_status_job.rb`
- `app/jobs/offboarding_message_job.rb`

**Impact**: All of these wrap their work in `if message.save … end` or call `user.update(...)` without `!`. Tests that only assert "happy path enqueues a downstream job" can pass while the failure branch silently swallows an invalid record.

**Recommendation**: For each job, add a test where `Message#save` (or `user.update`) returns `false` and assert that no downstream side-effect occurs *and* an error is reported to Appsignal (which today it isn't — see [JOB-01]).

### Low Severity

#### [TEST-03] `assert_see` helper hand-rolls HTML stripping

**File**: `test/test_helper.rb:54-65`

**Impact**: Custom regex over `@response.body` is brittle and likely to produce confusing failures. Capybara already exposes `have_text`. Worth replacing in incremental cleanups.

## 2. Code Quality Metrics

**Code Quality Metrics**: Not collected.

The RubyCritic subagent could not install or run because its `Edit Gemfile` and `bundle install`/`bundle exec rubycritic` tool calls were denied by the permission policy at audit time. To populate this section, re-run the audit and approve those tool calls.

In manual review the only file approaching the typical RubyCritic D/F threshold is `app/models/user.rb` (231 lines, 11 scopes, 13 public methods, mixed responsibilities). See [MODEL-01].

## 3. Security Issues

### High Severity

#### [SEC-01] PII excluded from `filter_parameters`

**File**: `config/initializers/filter_parameter_logging.rb:6-8`

**Impact**: `phone_number`, `postcode`, `child_name`, `child_birthday`, `first_name`, `referral_sources` flow through controller params and are logged in plaintext on every request. This is a personal-data-of-children product, and these fields are PII under UK GDPR. Anyone with access to production logs (or log archives) gets full PII.

**Current Code**:
```ruby
Rails.application.config.filter_parameters += [
  :passw, :email, :secret, :token, :_key, :crypt, :salt, :certificate, :otp, :ssn, :cvv, :cvc,
]
```

**Recommendation**:
```ruby
Rails.application.config.filter_parameters += [
  :passw, :email, :secret, :token, :_key, :crypt, :salt, :certificate, :otp, :ssn, :cvv, :cvc,
  :phone_number, :postcode, :child_name, :child_birthday, :first_name,
  :referral_sources, :Body, :From, :To,  # Twilio webhook payload also contains the message body
]
```

Twilio's `params["Body"]` (incoming message text) and `params["From"]` (sender phone) at `app/controllers/messages_controller.rb:21-22` are particularly important to filter — incoming SMS replies can contain the user's own personal context.

### Medium Severity

#### [SEC-02] Survey edit endpoint not scoped to user's own surveys

**File**: `app/controllers/surveys_controller.rb:32`

**Current Code**:
```ruby
def set_survey
  @survey = Survey.find(params[:id])
end

def set_user
  @user = User.find_by_token_for(:survey_token, params[:token])
  ...
end
```

**Impact**: A user with a valid `survey_token` can request `/surveys/:id/edit?token=…` for *any* survey id, not only the surveys their account has been sent. The `update` action then writes that survey's structure-attached answers back with `@user` as the author, but `@survey.update(survey_params)` also accepts `questions_attributes` containing `id`s — which is at least an information-disclosure vector (see anyone's question text), and at worst lets a user submit answers against a survey they were never sent.

**Recommendation**: Scope the lookup through the user's `SurveySend` records:
```ruby
def set_survey
  @survey = @user.surveys.find(params[:id])
end
```
(Set `@user` first.)

#### [SEC-03] `error.message.html_safe` / `error.capitalize.html_safe` in user form

**File**: `app/views/users/_form.html.erb:16, 29, 43, 67, 95`

**Impact**: Validation messages are interpolated as HTML so the `{{waitlist_link}}` substitution at line 67 can be rendered. That same pattern means *any* error message you ever add containing user-supplied data becomes XSS. Today the substituted content is admin-controlled HTML, but the pattern is fragile.

**Recommendation**: Render the link separately rather than `gsub`-ing into a translation, and drop `.html_safe` from the error rendering paths. Then validation messages are escaped by default again.

#### [SEC-04] `Status` callback only handles `failed`; non-failure status updates discarded

**File**: `app/controllers/messages_controller.rb:8-17`

**Impact**: Twilio sends `queued`, `sent`, `delivered`, `undelivered`, `failed`. Today only `failed` is forwarded to `UpdateMessageStatusJob`. If this is intentional, the dead branches in `UpdateMessageStatusJob` should be simplified; if not, you are silently dropping delivery confirmations and stuck-in-queued diagnostics.

**Recommendation**: If non-failure statuses matter, always enqueue the job and let it record the latest status. If only `failed` matters, narrow `UpdateMessageStatusJob` to only handle that case.

### Low Severity

#### [SEC-05] `Sessions#check_ip` IP allowlist is commented out

**File**: `app/controllers/sessions_controller.rb:2`

**Impact**: `prepend_before_action :check_ip` is dead. The dotted-out config and the env var (`ADMIN_IP_WHITELIST`) suggest an intended IP gate that no longer runs. Either delete the dead code or wire it back up — leaving it as a comment makes future readers think the gate exists.

---

## 4. Models Issues

### High Severity

#### [MODEL-01] Fat `User` model with mixed responsibilities

**File**: `app/models/user.rb` (231 lines)

**Impact**: One class owns: phone/postcode/age validation, language/group assignment, geocoding into a `LocalAuthority`, programme-progress tracking (next_content / finished_programme? / had_content_this_week?), waitlist lifecycle, anonymisation, and 11 query scopes. Several scopes embed dense `CASE WHEN … END` SQL. The class crosses the thoughtbot threshold for Large Class on three axes (lines, scopes, responsibilities).

**Recommendation**: Pull the programme-progress logic into a `UserProgramme` PORO, the waitlist logic into a `Waitlist` PORO with `ActiveModel::Model`, and move geocoding (`update_local_authority`) onto a `LocalAuthorities::Resolver` model. The scopes can stay on `User` but the heavy SQL ones (e.g. `with_four_messages_left`, `not_finished`) should move to scoped POROs in `app/models/users/`.

```ruby
# app/models/users/programme.rb
module Users
  class Programme
    def initialize(user) = @user = user
    def message_count = @user.messages.where.not(content_id: nil).count
    def finished? ... end
    def next_content ... end
  end
end
```

#### [MODEL-02] Voyeuristic / Painful Performance: `LocalAuthority#count_*` and `percentage_*`

**File**: `app/models/local_authority.rb:8-24`

**Current Code**:
```ruby
def count_users_by_created_at(timeframe)
  users
    .group_by { |user| user.created_at.strftime(timeframe) }
    .transform_values { |values| values.count }
end
```

**Impact**: Each method loads the entire users (or messages) collection for the LA into Ruby and then iterates with `group_by`. For an LA with thousands of users this materialises every record on every dashboard request — and `Admin::Dashboards::ClickThroughController` and `Admin::Dashboards::SignUpController` call these on every render. This is the canonical *Painful Performance* antipattern.

**Recommendation**: Push the group-by into SQL:
```ruby
def count_users_by_created_at(timeframe)
  users
    .group("to_char(created_at, '#{sql_format(timeframe)}')")
    .count
end
```
Where `sql_format` translates the Ruby strftime token to a Postgres `to_char` token. The materialized views in `db/schema.rb:454-538` already do exactly this for the dashboard summary stats — extend them to cover the per-day series instead of pulling everything into Ruby.

#### [MODEL-03] `Message#set_token` shadows the reader and can loop on collision

**File**: `app/models/message.rb:23-37`

**Current Code**:
```ruby
def generate_token
  self.token = SecureRandom.alphanumeric(6)
end

def set_token
  return unless new_record? && token.nil?
  token = generate_token            # <- assigns local var, shadowing reader
  while Message.exists?(token:)     # <- still reads the local var
    generate_token                  # <- updates self.token but not local var
  end
end
```

**Impact**: Once `token = generate_token` introduces a local, every subsequent `token` reference (including the keyword shorthand `token:` in `Message.exists?`) reads that local — never the freshly generated value. If the very first generated token collides, the loop is infinite. With 6-char alphanumeric the probability is small but real, and re-running `m.send(:generate_token)` from jobs (see [DESIGN-04]) makes pre-set tokens already-checked and the body of the loop never updates the variable being checked.

**Recommendation**: Drop the shadowing and use `Message.exists?(token: token)` against `self.token`, or just rely on the unique index:
```ruby
def set_token
  return unless new_record? && token.nil?
  loop do
    self.token = SecureRandom.alphanumeric(6)
    break unless Message.exists?(token: self.token)
  end
end
```

### Medium Severity

#### [MODEL-04] `Survey.trigger_for` filters in Ruby, not SQL

**File**: `app/models/survey.rb:18-20`

**Current Code**:
```ruby
matching = surveys.select do |survey|
  survey.send_after_message_count == message_count
end
```

**Recommendation**:
```ruby
surveys.where(send_after_message_count: message_count).find_each do |survey|
  SendSurveyJob.perform_later(user, survey)
end
```

This also drops a `surveys.where.not(...)` materialisation that was unused.

#### [MODEL-05] AR callbacks doing async work

**Files**:
- `app/models/content.rb:15` — `after_create :check_link_status` enqueues `CheckBbcLinksJob`, but the job iterates **all** active contents, not just the new one
- `app/models/message.rb:11` — `after_create :generate_reply, if: :received?` enqueues `ResponseMatcherJob`

**Impact**: Hidden side effects make tests (and incidents) harder. The `Content` callback also amplifies cost: every new Content kicks a job that re-checks every active link.

**Recommendation**: Move the enqueue out of the callback into the controller (`Admin::ContentsController#create`). For `CheckBbcLinksJob`, accept a `Content` argument so a new Content only checks itself.

#### [MODEL-06] `Admin#role` enum stored as plain string with no validation

**File**: `app/models/admin.rb:8`

**Current Code**:
```ruby
attribute :role, admin: "admin", local_authority: "local_authority"
```

**Impact**: `attribute` with a hash like this isn't a Rails enum (no helper methods, no validation). `application_controller.rb:14-19` then branches on `resource.role == "local_authority"` / `"admin"` — any typo elsewhere creates a silent fall-through that returns `nil` from `after_sign_in_path_for`.

**Recommendation**:
```ruby
enum :role, { admin: "admin", local_authority: "local_authority" }, prefix: true
validates :role, presence: true
```

#### [MODEL-07] `Group.find_by(language: ...)` assumes uniqueness with no index/validation

**File**: `app/models/user.rb:195` (`assign_group_by_language`) and `db/schema.rb` (`groups` table)

**Impact**: `User#assign_group_by_language` does `Group.find_by(language: language)` — assuming exactly one group per language. The `groups` table has no `unique` index on `language` and no validation enforces it. Inserting a second `en` group breaks user signup non-deterministically.

**Recommendation**: Add a unique index and validation:
```ruby
# Migration
add_index :groups, :language, unique: true
# Model
validates :language, presence: true, uniqueness: true
```

### Low Severity

#### [MODEL-08] `AutoResponse` JSON-validation error messages embed user input

**File**: `app/models/auto_response.rb:30`

**Impact**: `errors.add(attribute, "invalid field '#{field}' - not found in #{model.name} model")` interpolates `field` (parsed from admin-supplied JSON) into the error message, then [SEC-03] renders that string with `html_safe`. Admin-only entry, but combined with `html_safe` errors anywhere else, could become XSS.

---

## 5. Controllers Issues

### High Severity

#### [CTRL-01] Signup cap uses `==` instead of `>=`

**File**: `app/controllers/users_controller.rb:21`

**Current Code**:
```ruby
if User.where("created_at > ?", "2026-03-10").count == 3000
  return redirect_to root_path, notice: I18n.t("controllers.users.create.notice")
end
```

**Impact**: Two concurrent signups can both observe `count == 2999` and both succeed — pushing the count to 3001 — and from then on the cap **never triggers again**. A hard-coded `"2026-03-10"` ISO date string also bakes the boundary in code.

**Recommendation**:
```ruby
SIGNUP_CAP = 3000
SIGNUP_WINDOW_START = Date.new(2026, 3, 10)

if User.where(created_at: SIGNUP_WINDOW_START..).count >= SIGNUP_CAP
  return redirect_to root_path, notice: I18n.t("controllers.users.create.notice")
end
```
And if the cap matters under contention, gate it with a row-level advisory lock or an `UNIQUE` row-counter table — `count` + `if` is racy regardless of `==` vs `>=`.

#### [CTRL-02] `Admin::SurveySectionsController#create` has unreachable `else` branch

**File**: `app/controllers/admin/survey_sections_controller.rb:17-23`

**Current Code**:
```ruby
def create
  @survey_section = @survey.survey_sections.new(survey_section_params)
  if @survey_section.save!     # <- bang
    redirect_to admin_survey_path(@survey), notice: "Survey was successfully created."
  else
    render :new, status: :unprocessable_content
  end
end
```

**Impact**: `save!` raises on validation failure, so the `else` branch is dead and the admin sees a 500 (with full backtrace in dev) instead of the form re-rendered with errors.

**Recommendation**: Drop the bang: `if @survey_section.save`.

### Medium Severity

#### [CTRL-03] Admin user filter chains a misleading `where`

**File**: `app/controllers/admin/users_controller.rb:5-8`

**Current Code**:
```ruby
@users = User.where("phone_number ILIKE ?", "%#{params[:phone_number]}%")
  .where(params[:finished].present? ? {last_content_id: Content.order(:position).last&.id} : {})
  .where(params[:opted_out].present? ? {contactable: false} : {})
  .order(:first_name)
```

**Impact**: The "finished" filter only matches users whose `last_content_id` equals the single highest-positioned content across **all groups** — so it'll be wrong for any user not in the group whose last piece of content has the maximum position globally. Also `User#finished_programme?` (model) and "finished" (controller filter) use inconsistent definitions.

**Recommendation**: Move "finished" into a model scope that mirrors `User#finished_programme?` and call it from the controller. While there, reach for `params.permit(:phone_number, :finished, :opted_out)` to whitelist the filter inputs.

#### [CTRL-04] `Admin::Dashboards::BaseController#set_local_authority` blows up on missing `params[:q]`

**File**: `app/controllers/admin/dashboards/base_controller.rb:8-11`

**Current Code**:
```ruby
def set_local_authority
  name = params[:q].split("_").map { |word| NOCAPS.include?(word) ? word : word.capitalize }.join(" ")
  @local_authority = LocalAuthority.find_by(name:)
end
```

**Impact**: `params[:q]` is required for the action to make sense, but if it ever arrives missing/`nil` you get a 500 (`NoMethodError: undefined method 'split' for nil`). Also the round-trip through string capitalisation is a brittle way to identify a record — pass `local_authority_id` instead.

**Recommendation**:
```ruby
before_action :require_q

def set_local_authority
  @local_authority = LocalAuthority.find(params[:local_authority_id])
end

private

def require_q
  head :bad_request if params[:local_authority_id].blank?
end
```

#### [CTRL-05] Repeated direct `.find(params[:id])` in admin namespace

**Files**: 14 sites (see `grep -rn '\.find(params\[:id\])' app/controllers/`)

**Impact**: Not exploitable today (admins are trusted) but a missing record returns a 404 ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound trace where `find_by(id: …)` + redirect_to with notice would be friendlier. Several actions (e.g. `Admin::GroupsController#edit/update/destroy`) re-fetch the same record per action instead of using the existing `set_*` callback pattern.

**Recommendation**: Introduce a `before_action :set_<thing>` per controller and remove the repeated `.find(params[:id])` calls.

### Low Severity

#### [CTRL-06] Inconsistent strong-params across admin controllers

**File**: `app/controllers/admin/users_controller.rb:32-34` permits only `:contactable`, while there's no audit of who can set `programme_length`, `language`, `local_authority_id` from the admin UI. If admins are supposed to edit those, add them; if not, keep as-is but document it.

---

## 6. Code Design Issues

### High Severity

#### [DESIGN-01] Surveys looked up by mutable English title

**Files**:
- `app/controllers/users_controller.rb:75` — `Survey.find_by(title_en: "Pre-programme survey")`
- `app/jobs/send_bulk_message_job.rb:24` — same
- `app/jobs/offboarding_message_job.rb:6` — `Survey.find_by(title_en: "Offboarding")`

**Impact**: Three independent code paths identify a survey by its admin-editable English title. Renaming a survey in `Admin::SurveysController#edit` silently disables onboarding, the survey-reminder job, and offboarding messaging. There is no admin UI warning when this happens.

**Recommendation**: Add a stable `code` column with a unique index:
```ruby
# db/migrate
add_column :surveys, :code, :string
add_index :surveys, :code, unique: true
# app/models/survey.rb
validates :code, presence: true, uniqueness: true
```
And replace each lookup with `Survey.find_by!(code: "pre_programme")` etc. The `find_by!` ensures missing codes fail loudly in deploy validation rather than at message-send time.

### Medium Severity

#### [DESIGN-02] `app/services/` houses two service objects

**Files**:
- `app/services/postcode_service.rb` — single class method `valid_welsh_postcode?`
- `app/services/response_matcher_service.rb` — `match_response` orchestrating auto-responses
- `app/services/twilio/client.rb` — wrapper around the Twilio gem

**Recommendation per thoughtbot Ruby Science**:
- `PostcodeService` is one class method — turn into `Postcode.valid_welsh?(string)` in `app/models/postcode.rb`. (One predicate isn't worth a value object, but at least drop the `Service` suffix.)
- `ResponseMatcherService` is genuinely a domain object — rename to `app/models/auto_response_matcher.rb` (the noun). Method `#match_response` → `#match!`. Add `ActiveModel::Model` for consistency.
- `Twilio::Client` is a thin client wrapper, the `Service` suffix is absent — that's fine. Consider moving to `app/lib/twilio/client.rb` to signal "infrastructure adapter" rather than domain code.

#### [DESIGN-03] `SendBulkMessageJob#perform` is a 30-line `case` statement

**File**: `app/jobs/send_bulk_message_job.rb:4-33`

**Impact**: One job dispatches to seven different message-type flows via a `case` on `message_type`. Each branch is structurally identical: pick a User scope, map to job class, `perform_all_later`. This is a *Type Code* smell.

**Recommendation**: Extract a registry / dispatch table:
```ruby
DISPATCH = {
  "weekly_message"  => ->(time) { [users_for(time), SendMessageJob] },
  "bilingual_text"  => -> { [User.contactable.received_six_messages_without_bilingual_text, SendBilingualMessageJob] },
  "feedback"        => -> { [User.contactable.received_two_or_eighteen_messages, SendFeedbackMessageJob] },
  ...
}
```
Or split into one job per message type and let the cron schedule pick.

#### [DESIGN-04] `m.send(:generate_token)` repeated in 7 jobs, despite `before_validation :set_token`

**Files**: `app/jobs/send_*_job.rb` (7 jobs)

**Impact**:
1. `Message#generate_token` is *public* (defined above `private` on line 27 of `message.rb`), so `send(:generate_token)` is unnecessary;
2. `Message#set_token` already runs `before_validation`, generating a token if one isn't set, so the manual generation in jobs is redundant;
3. But because the jobs *do* set `m.token` manually, `set_token` short-circuits (the `token.nil?` guard returns early), and the collision-check loop never runs at all for these messages.

**Recommendation**: Remove the manual `m.token = m.send(:generate_token)` line from all 7 jobs. Trust the model's `before_validation`. After [MODEL-03] is fixed, the model handles uniqueness too.

#### [DESIGN-05] `SendCustomMessageJob` is a single-line wrapper that's bypassed inconsistently

**File**: `app/jobs/send_custom_message_job.rb`

**Impact**: `SendWelcomeMessageJob` and `Twilio::Client.new.send_message` calls bypass the wrapper; bulk message paths and the response-matcher use it. Either the wrapper has a purpose (queue/retry config) or it's dead weight.

**Recommendation**: Either route every Twilio send through it (and add the retry/throttling logic that justifies it), or inline it.

#### [DESIGN-06] `app/lib/geokit/geocoders/application_geocoder.rb#parse_json` has dead branch

**File**: `app/lib/geokit/geocoders/application_geocoder.rb:21-29`

**Current Code**:
```ruby
def self.parse_json(results)
  return GeoLoc.new unless results["features"].count > 0
  loc = nil

  extracted_geoloc = extract_geoloc(results["features"].first)
  if loc.nil?
    loc = extracted_geoloc
  else
    loc.all.push(extracted_geoloc)  # unreachable
  end
  loc
end
```

The `else` branch can never run (`loc` was just set to `nil`).

**Recommendation**: Collapse to `extract_geoloc(results["features"].first)`.

### Low Severity

#### [DESIGN-07] `LocationGeocoder` retries silently and returns `nil` on exhaustion

**File**: `app/lib/location_geocoder.rb:9-14`

**Impact**: When all 3 attempts fail, the method returns `nil`. Callers (`User#update_local_authority`, `PostcodeService.valid_welsh_postcode?`) handle `nil` differently and neither reports the geocoder being unreachable to Appsignal.

**Recommendation**: Report the final exhaustion to Appsignal and let the caller decide whether to treat it as "no LA assigned" or "fail signup".

#### [DESIGN-08] Repeated `.gsub("{{link}}", ...)` and `.gsub("{{survey_url}}", ...)` outside `MessageVariableSubstitution`

**Files**: `restart_messages_job.rb:10`, `offboarding_message_job.rb:12`, `send_survey_job.rb:14`, `send_survey_reminder_job.rb:14`, `users/thank_you.html.erb:27`

**Recommendation**: Add `{{link}}` and `{{survey_url}}` to `MessageVariableSubstitution#substitute_variables` and use it consistently.

---

## 7. Views Issues

### Medium Severity

#### [VIEW-01] PHPitis: model queries in admin dashboard

**File**: `app/views/admin/dashboards/show.html.erb:65-99, 113`

**Current Code**:
```erb
<% LocalAuthority.most_users_order.limit(5).each do |la| %> ... <% end %>
<% LocalAuthority.where(country: "England").most_users_order.limit(5).each do |la| %> ... <% end %>
<% LocalAuthority.where(country: "Scotland").most_users_order.limit(5).each do |la| %> ... <% end %>
<% LocalAuthority.where(country: "Wales").most_users_order.limit(5).each do |la| %> ... <% end %>
<% LocalAuthority.where(country: "Northern Ireland").most_users_order.limit(5).each do |la| %> ... <% end %>
<% LocalAuthority.all.order(:name).each do |la| %> ... <% end %>
```

**Impact**: Six independent DB queries inlined into the template. Each runs on every dashboard render. Logic is duplicated for the four UK nations.

**Recommendation**: Move to the controller:
```ruby
class Admin::DashboardsController < ApplicationController
  TOP_BY_COUNTRY = %w[England Scotland Wales] + ["Northern Ireland"]

  def show
    @top_overall = LocalAuthority.most_users_order.limit(5)
    @top_by_country = TOP_BY_COUNTRY.index_with do |c|
      LocalAuthority.where(country: c).most_users_order.limit(5)
    end
    @all_authorities = LocalAuthority.order(:name)
    ...
  end
end
```
And iterate `@top_by_country.each do |country, las|` in the view.

#### [VIEW-02] `surveys/edit.html.erb` mixes `sanitize` and `html_safe` redundantly

**File**: `app/views/surveys/edit.html.erb:29-30, 39-41, 53-55`

**Impact**: `sanitize @language == "cy" ? q.object.text_cy.html_safe : q.object.text_en.html_safe` — `sanitize` already returns html-safe output and itself escapes anything it doesn't whitelist, so the inner `.html_safe` calls are noise (and potentially confusing should the chain ever change). Same shape repeated 6 times.

**Recommendation**: `sanitize(localised_text(q))` where `localised_text` is a helper that returns the right column.

### Low Severity

#### [VIEW-03] Translations rendered with `html_safe`

**File**: `app/views/users/thank_you.html.erb:22, 27`, all of `app/views/pages/*.html.erb`

**Impact**: Translation files are admin-controlled, so this is acceptable. The risk is the *next* engineer reading these files learns the pattern "errors are safe, translate-and-html_safe-everything". Add a comment or wrap them in `sanitize` for defence-in-depth.

---

## 8. External Services Issues

### High Severity

#### [JOB-01] Multiple jobs swallow `update`/`save` failures silently

**Files**:
- `app/jobs/nudge_users_job.rb:11` — `user.update(nudged_at: Time.zone.now)`
- `app/jobs/restart_messages_job.rb:7` — `if user.update(contactable: true, restart_at: nil)` (false case has no Appsignal call)
- `app/jobs/send_bilingual_message_job.rb:20` — `user.update(sent_bilingual_text_at: Time.zone.now)`
- `app/jobs/send_feedback_message_job.rb:13` — `user.update(asked_for_feedback: true)`
- `app/jobs/update_message_status_job.rb:12` — `message.update(status:)`
- `app/services/twilio/client.rb:21,27` — `message.update(status: ...)`
- `app/services/response_matcher_service.rb:58` — `object.update(key => value)` where `key` comes from admin JSON

**Impact**: Each call returns `false` on validation failure. Without `.update!` or a return-value check, the job/job-step "succeeds" while the data write is lost. `ResponseMatcherService#update_attribute` is the most dangerous — admin-controlled `update_user` JSON sends invalid keys silently into nothing.

**Recommendation**: Use `update!` and let the job fail (jobs already retry):
```ruby
user.update!(nudged_at: Time.zone.now)
```
Or, when the failure is expected and recoverable, log to Appsignal explicitly:
```ruby
Appsignal.report_error(...) unless user.update(...)
```

### Medium Severity

#### [JOB-02] `AnonymiseUsersJob` uses bare `rescue => e`

**File**: `app/jobs/anonymise_users_job.rb:8`

**Current Code**:
```ruby
rescue => e
  Appsignal.report_error(e)
end
```

**Impact**: `rescue =>` (no class) catches `StandardError`. Subclasses will swallow programmer bugs (`NoMethodError`, `ArgumentError`) at the top level of the job. The Appsignal report makes this less bad than `rescue nil`, but you'd rather the job re-raise so the queue retries (or surfaces the bug in the failed-jobs view).

**Recommendation**: Either let `User#anonymise!` already-handles-its-own-errors do the work and remove the outer rescue, or narrow the rescue to expected `ActiveRecord::ActiveRecordError`.

#### [JOB-03] `Twilio::Client#send_message` uses `message.update(...)` without bang

**File**: `app/services/twilio/client.rb:21, 27`

**Recommendation**: `message.update!(status: sms.status, message_sid: sms.sid)` — if Twilio sent the SMS but we couldn't record it locally, Appsignal needs to scream, not stay silent.

---

## 9. Database & Performance Issues

### High Severity

#### [DB-01] Migrations reference application models directly

**Files**:
- `db/migrate/20260326140858_add_group_reference_to_user.rb:5-8` — `User.find_each` + `Group.order(...)`
- `db/migrate/20260325091819_add_more_fields_to_user.rb:7-10` — `User.find_each` + reading `user.referral_source` (which is `remove_column`'d below in the same migration)
- (others under `grep -rln 'User\.\|Group\.\|Survey\.' db/migrate/`)

**Impact**: When `User` validates new attributes, `User.find_each` in a year-old migration breaks `db:migrate` for fresh checkouts. The `20260325091819` example is worse — it removes a column it depends on inside the same migration; running it on an empty DB now would still work, but the data step is no-op (no users), and the next time someone runs it on a hot fork it could break.

**Recommendation**: Use raw SQL or define an inline anonymous model:
```ruby
class AddGroupReferenceToUser < ActiveRecord::Migration[8.1]
  class User < ApplicationRecord; end
  class Group < ApplicationRecord; end

  def up
    add_reference :users, :group, null: true, foreign_key: true
    execute <<~SQL
      UPDATE users
         SET group_id = (SELECT id FROM groups ORDER BY created_at LIMIT 1)
    SQL
    change_column_null :users, :group_id, false
  end

  def down
    remove_reference :users, :group, foreign_key: true
  end
end
```

#### [DB-02] In-Ruby aggregation in `LocalAuthority` (see [MODEL-02])

Already covered above — duplicated here because it's both a model and a database/perf concern.

### Medium Severity

#### [DB-03] Missing unique index/validation on `groups.language`

See [MODEL-07]. Add:
```ruby
add_index :groups, :language, unique: true
```

#### [DB-04] No index on `auto_responses.trigger_phrase` despite primary lookup

**File**: `db/schema.rb:107-114`, `app/services/response_matcher_service.rb:8`

**Impact**: `AutoResponse.where(trigger_phrase: normalized_message_body)` runs on every incoming SMS. The table is small today but the query is O(n) without an index.

**Recommendation**:
```ruby
add_index :auto_responses, :trigger_phrase
```

#### [DB-05] `messages.user_id` allows NULL in schema; model declares `belongs_to :user` (required)

**File**: `db/schema.rb:219` (`t.bigint "user_id"` — no `null: false`); `app/models/message.rb:2`

**Impact**: Model enforces presence at the application layer, but the DB will accept a row with `user_id IS NULL` if any path bypasses validation. `MessagesController#incoming` actually creates messages with `user: nil` when a phone number isn't recognised (`User.find_by(phone_number: params["From"])` returns `nil`). So today the column **does** nullably contain rows for unknown senders.

If that's intentional, mark the association `optional: true`:
```ruby
belongs_to :user, optional: true
```
If not, change `MessagesController#incoming` to drop unknown-sender messages instead of saving them with `user_id: nil`, then add `change_column_null :messages, :user_id, false`.

#### [DB-06] Materialized views refresh non-concurrently

**Files**: `app/models/all_las_dashboard.rb:3`, `app/models/la_specific_dashboard.rb:3`

**Current Code**:
```ruby
Scenic.database.refresh_materialized_view(table_name, concurrently: false, cascade: false)
```

**Impact**: `concurrently: false` takes an `ACCESS EXCLUSIVE` lock, blocking dashboard reads during refresh. If the refresh runs during business hours, admins see hangs.

**Recommendation**: Add a unique index to each materialized view (Postgres requires it for `REFRESH … CONCURRENTLY`) and switch to `concurrently: true`.

---

## Recommendations Summary

### Quick Wins (Immediate Action)

1. [x] [SEC-01] Add `:phone_number, :postcode, :child_name, :child_birthday, :first_name, :Body, :From` to `filter_parameters`.
2. [x] [CTRL-01] Change `== 3000` to `>= SIGNUP_CAP` in `users_controller.rb:21` and pull the constant out.
3. [x] [CTRL-02] Drop the `!` from `@survey_section.save!` in `Admin::SurveySectionsController#create`.
4. [x] [DESIGN-04] Remove the redundant `m.token = m.send(:generate_token)` line from all 7 send_*_job.rb files.
5. [x] [MODEL-03] Fix the local-variable shadowing in `Message#set_token`.
6. [ ] [DESIGN-06] Inline `extract_geoloc(...)` and delete the dead `else` branch in `ApplicationGeocoder.parse_json`.
7. [ ] [SEC-05] Either delete or restore `Sessions#check_ip`.

### Short-term (This Sprint)

1. [ ] [DESIGN-01] Introduce `Survey#code` and migrate the three `find_by(title_en:)` call sites.
2. [ ] [VIEW-01] Move the six dashboard queries out of `admin/dashboards/show.html.erb` into the controller.
3. [ ] [JOB-01] Convert `update`/`save` to `update!`/`save!` (or explicit Appsignal reports) across the eight jobs/services flagged.
4. [ ] [MODEL-02] Replace Ruby `group_by` in `LocalAuthority` with SQL `GROUP BY` (or extend the materialized view).
5. [ ] [MODEL-07] / [DB-03] Add unique index + validation on `groups.language`.
6. [ ] [SEC-02] Scope `SurveysController#set_survey` to `@user.surveys`.
7. [ ] [DB-04] Add index on `auto_responses.trigger_phrase`.

### Long-term (Technical Debt)

1. [ ] [MODEL-01] Decompose `User` into `Users::Programme`, `Waitlist`, `Anonymisation`, etc.
2. [ ] [DESIGN-02] Rename `*Service` files to domain models in `app/models/`.
3. [ ] [DESIGN-03] Replace the `SendBulkMessageJob#perform` `case` with a registry or one-job-per-type.
4. [ ] [DB-01] Audit and rewrite the model-referencing migrations to use raw SQL or inline anonymous models, to make `db:migrate` from scratch reliable again.
5. [ ] [DB-05] Decide whether `messages.user_id` is nullable; if not, backfill and add `null: false`.
6. [ ] Add request specs for `pages_controller`, `admin/dashboards/sign_up_controller`, `admin/dashboards/click_through_controller`, `admin/dashboards_controller`.

---

## Files Analyzed

| Directory | Files Analyzed | Issues Found |
|-----------|----------------|--------------|
| app/models/ | 17 | 8 |
| app/controllers/ | 17 (6 public + 11 admin) | 6 |
| app/services/ | 3 | 3 (covered in Design + Jobs) |
| app/jobs/ | 19 | 5 |
| app/views/ | 62 ERB files | 3 |
| app/lib/ | 2 | 2 (covered in Design) |
| app/helpers/ | 1 | 0 |
| db/migrate/ | 81 | ~10 reference application models (one specific issue: [DB-01]) |
| db/schema.rb | 1 | 4 |
| config/ | sampled | 1 ([SEC-01]) |
| test/ | 56 test files | 3 |
| **Total** | **~260** | **42** |

## Appendix: Tools Recommendations

For ongoing code quality, this project already has Brakeman, RuboCop (with standard / standard-rails), Bullet, and bundler-audit lookalikes available. Worth adding:

1. **SimpleCov** — was *intended* to be collected for this audit but the setup was blocked at runtime; add it to `Gemfile :test` permanently. With Minitest:
   ```ruby
   # Gemfile
   group :test do
     gem "simplecov", require: false
   end
   # test/test_helper.rb (very top)
   require "simplecov"
   SimpleCov.start "rails" do
     enable_coverage :branch
   end
   ```
2. **RubyCritic** — also intended, also blocked. Adding it as a `:development` gem and running `bundle exec rubycritic app lib --no-browser` periodically catches drift.
3. **strong_migrations** — given [DB-01], strong_migrations would have flagged the data-changes-in-`change` migrations and the `change_column_null` against existing rows.
4. **database_consistency** — would have flagged [DB-03] (unique-index missing for `validates :uniqueness`) and [DB-05] (NOT NULL mismatch between model and column).
5. **Bullet** — already present; ensure it's enabled in `test` env so any newly introduced N+1 from [VIEW-01] / [MODEL-02] gets caught.
